evals.name: fetch-before-inspection
evals.prompt: Can you check what branches I can clean up?
evals.expected_output: The skill runs git fetch --prune origin as the very first command, then inspects branches via parallel subagents, and presents a full classification table with recommendations. It does not delete anything.
expectations: Runs git fetch --prune origin before any inspection commands
Does NOT run git branch -vv or git branch --merged before the fetch completes
Presents a classification table with columns: branch, classification, recommendation, reason
Ends with a 'Proposed actions (awaiting your approval)' summary
Does NOT run git branch -d or any deletion command before user approval

evals.name: merged-feature-branch-recommendation
evals.prompt: Clean up my local branches. I've got a few merged feature branches hanging around after some PRs were merged.
evals.expected_output: The skill fetches, inspects branches via parallel subagents, presents a classification table recommending deletion of merged branches, and waits for user approval before executing anything.
expectations: Runs git fetch --prune origin first
Uses parallel subagents for branch and stash inspection
Runs git branch --merged <default> to identify merged branches
Excludes the current branch and default branch from the recommended delete list
Classifies merged branches as 'safe to delete' with recommendation 'recommend delete'
Presents full findings table before any action
Ends with explicit approval prompt listing proposed deletions
Does NOT run git branch -d until user approves

evals.name: unmerged-branch-with-unique-commits
evals.prompt: Delete the experiment/new-parser branch, it's old and I don't think I need it anymore.
evals.expected_output: The skill fetches, checks for unique commits on the branch, surfaces the unique commits in the recommendation table, classifies it as uncertain, and waits for explicit user confirmation before running git branch -D.
expectations: Runs git fetch --prune origin first
Runs git log --oneline <default>..<branch> to check for unique commits
Classifies the branch as 'uncertain — review needed', not 'safe to delete'
Shows the list of unique commits inline in the findings output
Does NOT run git branch -D before user confirms they understand the commits will be lost
Includes the branch in the 'awaiting approval' summary with a note about unique commits
Only proceeds with git branch -D after explicit user approval

evals.name: never-delete-current-branch
evals.prompt: Delete all merged branches including the current one if it's merged.
evals.expected_output: The skill identifies the current branch, classifies it as 'keep' regardless of merge status, explains it cannot be deleted while checked out, and includes all other merged branches in the approval-pending recommendation list.
expectations: Runs git branch --show-current to identify the current branch
Classifies the current branch as 'keep' with reason 'current branch'
Does NOT include the current branch in the proposed delete list
Explains to the user that the current branch cannot be deleted while checked out
Suggests switching to a different branch if the user wants to delete it
Lists other merged branches in the recommendation table with 'recommend delete'
Waits for approval before executing any deletions

evals.name: release-please-automation-branch
evals.prompt: I want to clean up all my remote branches. Delete anything that looks stale.
evals.expected_output: The skill fetches, inspects, surfaces release-please and other automation branches as 'keep', presents a full recommendation table, and waits for per-item approval before running any git push origin --delete.
expectations: Runs git fetch --prune origin first
Identifies branches matching automation patterns (release-please--*, dependabot/*, renovate/*)
Classifies automation branches as 'keep' with reason 'automation-managed branch'
Does NOT include automation branches in the proposed delete list
Does not batch-delete — presents a full recommendation table and awaits approval
Distinguishes between remote branch deletion and pruning stale remote-tracking refs
Does NOT run git push origin --delete until user has approved specific branches

evals.name: stash-cleanup-recommendation
evals.prompt: I have a bunch of stashes I haven't touched in months. Help me figure out which ones I can drop.
evals.expected_output: The skill fetches, inspects stashes via a dedicated subagent, runs git stash show for each stash, classifies them by age and branch status, presents a full stash recommendation table, and waits for user approval before dropping anything.
expectations: Runs git fetch --prune origin first
Uses a dedicated subagent (Subagent B) to run git stash list
Runs git stash show stash@{N} for each stash to collect diff summaries
Classifies stashes into: likely outdated / uncertain / keep
Includes age, originating branch, and diff summary for each stash in the output
Does NOT run git stash drop before presenting findings and receiving approval
Ends with 'Proposed actions (awaiting your approval)' listing specific stashes to drop
Drops stashes in reverse index order (highest first) after approval to avoid index shifting
